ucs.title: [LLM] PlushDaemon SlowStepper sideload chain — signed-binary loader pulling stagers from gitee/GitHub
ucs.description: Hunts the SlowStepper backdoor execution pattern PlushDaemon (ESET-tracked China-nexus actor) used in its 2024-2025 IPany VPN supply-chain compromise: a legitimate signed binary (often renamed/hijacked) sideloads a malicious DLL (e.g. AutoMsg.dll, lregdll.dll, EncMgr.dll, PerfOS.dll) from a user-writable directory such as %PROGRAMDATA%\Apex, then beacons out to gitee.com / raw.githubusercontent.com / gitee.io to fetch the next-stage Python modules. The combination of sideload-from-non-system-path + egress to public code-hosting in the same process is highly characteristic — generic LOLBin or g
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parent values(Processes.process_path) as image from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process_path IN ("*\\ProgramData\\Apex\\*","*\\Users\\Public\\*","*\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\*","*\\ProgramData\\McAfee\\*") OR Processes.process_name IN ("svcghost.exe","PerfWatson.exe","IPanyVPN.exe","EncMgr.exe","AutoMsg.exe")) AND Processes.user!="*$" by host Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.process_id Processes.process_name | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | join type=inner dest process_id [ | tstats summariesonly=t count values(All_Traffic.dest) as c2_host values(All_Traffic.dest_ip) as c2_ip from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic where (All_Traffic.dest IN ("*gitee.com","*gitee.io","*githubusercontent.com","*gitcode.com","*codeberg.org")) by All_Traffic.src All_Traffic.process_id | rename All_Traffic.src as dest All_Traffic.process_id as process_id ] | where (lastTime - firstTime) < 900 | table firstTime dest user process_name image cmdline parent c2_host c2_ip
ucs.kql:
// PlushDaemon SlowStepper sideload + code-hosting C2 within 10m on the same process
let Lookback = 14d;
let StageDirs = dynamic([@"\ProgramData\Apex\", @"\ProgramData\McAfee\", @"\Users\Public\", @"\AppData\Local\Temp\", @"\AppData\Roaming\"]);
let SuspectModules = dynamic(["AutoMsg.dll","lregdll.dll","EncMgr.dll","PerfOS.dll","svcghost.dll"]);
let CodeHosts = dynamic(["gitee.com","gitee.io","raw.githubusercontent.com","gitcode.com","codeberg.org"]);
let Sideloads =
    DeviceImageLoadEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(Lookback)
    | where InitiatingProcessAccountName !endswith "$"
    // signed-looking exe loading a DLL from a user-writable stage dir, OR known SlowStepper module names
    | where (FolderPath has_any (StageDirs) and not(FolderPath startswith @"C:\Windows\")) or FileName in~ (SuspectModules)
    | where InitiatingProcessFolderPath has_any (StageDirs)  // loader itself relocated outside Program Files / System32
    | project LoadTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName,
              InitiatingProcessAccountName, InitiatingProcessId,
              InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessFolderPath,
              InitiatingProcessSHA256, LoadedDll = strcat(FolderPath, FileName);
DeviceNetworkEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(Lookback)
| where RemoteUrl has_any (CodeHosts)
| where InitiatingProcessAccountName !endswith "$"
| project NetTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, RemoteUrl, RemoteIP, RemotePort,
          InitiatingProcessId, NetProcess = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          NetCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine
| join kind=inner Sideloads on DeviceId, InitiatingProcessId
| where NetTime between (LoadTime .. LoadTime + 10m)
| project LoadTime, NetTime, DeviceName, InitiatingProcessAccountName,
          Loader = InitiatingProcessFileName, LoaderPath = InitiatingProcessFolderPath,
          LoaderSHA256 = InitiatingProcessSHA256, LoadedDll,
          NetProcess, NetCmd, RemoteUrl, RemoteIP, RemotePort
| order by LoadTime desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Sentinel: ImageLoad telemetry isn't uniformly normalised, so anchor on Sysmon EID 7 if present + ImNetworkSession egress
let Lookback = 14d;
let StageDirs = dynamic([@"\ProgramData\Apex\", @"\ProgramData\McAfee\", @"\Users\Public\", @"\AppData\Local\Temp\"]);
let SuspectModules = dynamic(["AutoMsg.dll","lregdll.dll","EncMgr.dll","PerfOS.dll"]);
let CodeHosts = dynamic(["gitee.com","gitee.io","raw.githubusercontent.com","gitcode.com","codeberg.org"]);
let Sideloads =
    WindowsEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(Lookback)
    | where Provider == "Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon" and EventID == 7   // ImageLoad
    | extend Image = tostring(EventData.Image),
             ImageLoaded = tostring(EventData.ImageLoaded),
             ProcessId = toint(EventData.ProcessId),
             User = tostring(EventData.User)
    | where User !endswith "$"
    | where (ImageLoaded has_any (StageDirs) and not(ImageLoaded startswith @"C:\Windows\"))
         or (ImageLoaded has_any (SuspectModules))
    | where Image has_any (StageDirs)
    | project LoadTime = TimeGenerated, Computer, User, Image, ImageLoaded, ProcessId;
ImNetworkSession
| where TimeGenerated > ago(Lookback)
| where Url has_any (CodeHosts) or DstHostname has_any (CodeHosts)
| where SrcUsername !endswith "$"
| project NetTime = TimeGenerated, DvcHostname, SrcUsername = SrcUsername,
          NetProcess = ActingProcessName, NetCmd = ActingProcessCommandLine,
          ProcessId = toint(ActingProcessId), Url, DstHostname, DstIpAddr
| join kind=inner Sideloads on $left.DvcHostname == $right.Computer, ProcessId
| where NetTime between (LoadTime .. LoadTime + 10m)
| project LoadTime, NetTime, DvcHostname, User, Loader = Image, LoadedDll = ImageLoaded,
          NetProcess, NetCmd, Url, DstHostname, DstIpAddr
ucs.rationale: PlushDaemon's SlowStepper backdoor (documented by ESET in the IPany VPN supply-chain incident, Jan 2025) is built around DLL side-loading from non-standard directories (Apex / Public / Temp), with the loader pulling its Python module set from gitee.com and GitHub raw — gitee.com specifically is a strong China-nexus tell. Either signal alone is noisy (lots of devs hit GitHub raw; lots of installers

_sentinel_field_issues.message: `ActorUsername` is not a column of ImNetworkSession, WindowsEvent; it does exist on ImProcessCreate, ImAuthentication, ImFileEvent, ImRegistryEvent.
`ActingProcessName` is not a column of ImNetworkSession, WindowsEvent; it does exist on ImProcessCreate, ImFileEvent, ImRegistryEvent.
`ActingProcessCommandLine` is not a column of ImNetworkSession, WindowsEvent; it does exist on ImProcessCreate, ImFileEvent, ImRegistryEvent.
`ActingProcessId` is not a column of ImNetworkSession, WindowsEvent; it does exist on ImProcessCreate, ImFileEvent, ImRegistryEvent.

ucs.title: [LLM] PlushDaemon hijacked-installer persistence — supply-chain dropper writing to %PROGRAMDATA%\Apex with scheduled-task/Run-key persistence
ucs.description: Hunts PlushDaemon's IPany-style supply-chain delivery pattern: a hijacked legitimate installer (VPN, KR/CN-vendor software, or update binary) writes the SlowStepper artifact set (svcghost.exe / AutoMsg.dll / EncMgr.dll / lregdll.dll, often staged under %PROGRAMDATA%\Apex) and within an hour registers persistence via a Run/RunOnce value or a scheduled task pointing at that staging directory. The actor characteristically reuses the same Apex/svcghost naming across victims, so the join between file-drop and persistence on a known path is a near-zero-FP signature.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as dropTime values(Filesystem.file_path) as dropped_path values(Filesystem.process_name) as dropper from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where (Filesystem.file_path IN ("*\\ProgramData\\Apex\\*","*\\ProgramData\\McAfee\\*","*\\Users\\Public\\Documents\\*") OR Filesystem.file_name IN ("svcghost.exe","AutoMsg.dll","EncMgr.dll","lregdll.dll","PerfOS.dll")) by host Filesystem.dest Filesystem.user Filesystem.file_name Filesystem.file_hash | `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)` | join type=inner dest [ | tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as persistTime values(Registry.registry_key_name) as reg_key values(Registry.registry_value_data) as reg_value from datamodel=Endpoint.Registry where (Registry.registry_key_name="*\\Run*" OR Registry.registry_key_name="*\\Image File Execution Options\\*") AND (Registry.registry_value_data="*Apex*" OR Registry.registry_value_data="*svcghost*" OR Registry.registry_value_data="*EncMgr*" OR Registry.registry_value_data="*PerfWatson*") by Registry.dest | rename Registry.dest as dest ] | where persistTime - dropTime < 3600 AND persistTime >= dropTime | table dropTime persistTime dest user file_name file_hash dropped_path dropper reg_key reg_value
ucs.kql:
// PlushDaemon hijacked-installer drop + same-host persistence within 60 min
let Lookback = 30d;
let StageDirs = dynamic([@"\ProgramData\Apex\", @"\ProgramData\McAfee\", @"\Users\Public\Documents\"]);
let SlowStepperArtifacts = dynamic(["svcghost.exe","AutoMsg.dll","EncMgr.dll","lregdll.dll","PerfOS.dll","PerfWatson.exe"]);
let PersistMarkers = dynamic(["Apex","svcghost","EncMgr","PerfWatson","AutoMsg"]);
let Drops =
    DeviceFileEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(Lookback)
    | where ActionType in ("FileCreated","FileRenamed")
    | where (FolderPath has_any (StageDirs)) or (FileName in~ (SlowStepperArtifacts))
    | where InitiatingProcessAccountName !endswith "$"
    // hijacked-installer heuristic — dropper is an installer/setup binary or runs from Temp
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName has_any ("setup","install","vpn","ipany","update")
         or InitiatingProcessFolderPath has_any (@"\AppData\Local\Temp\", @"\Windows\Temp\", @"\Downloads\")
    | project DropTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName,
              DroppedFile = strcat(FolderPath, FileName), FileSHA256 = SHA256,
              Dropper = InitiatingProcessFileName, DropperPath = InitiatingProcessFolderPath,
              DropperCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
              InitiatingProcessAccountName;
let Persist =
    union
    ( DeviceRegistryEvents
      | where Timestamp > ago(Lookback)
      | where ActionType == "RegistryValueSet"
      | where RegistryKey has_any (@"\CurrentVersion\Run", @"\CurrentVersion\RunOnce", @"\Image File Execution Options\")
      | where RegistryValueData has_any (PersistMarkers)
      | project PersistTime = Timestamp, DeviceId,
                PersistType = "Registry",
                PersistDetail = strcat(RegistryKey, "::", RegistryValueName, " = ", RegistryValueData) ),
    ( DeviceProcessEvents
      | where Timestamp > ago(Lookback)
      | where FileName =~ "schtasks.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has "/create"
      | where ProcessCommandLine has_any (PersistMarkers)
      | project PersistTime = Timestamp, DeviceId,
                PersistType = "ScheduledTask",
                PersistDetail = ProcessCommandLine );
Drops
| join kind=inner Persist on DeviceId
| where PersistTime between (DropTime .. DropTime + 1h)
| project DropTime, PersistTime,
          MinutesDelta = datetime_diff('minute', PersistTime, DropTime),
          DeviceName, InitiatingProcessAccountName,
          DroppedFile, FileSHA256, Dropper, DropperPath, DropperCmd,
          PersistType, PersistDetail
| order by DropTime desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Sentinel: file drop via ImFileEvent + persistence via ImRegistryEvent or schtasks process-create
let Lookback = 30d;
let StageDirs = dynamic([@"\ProgramData\Apex\", @"\ProgramData\McAfee\", @"\Users\Public\Documents\"]);
let SlowStepperArtifacts = dynamic(["svcghost.exe","AutoMsg.dll","EncMgr.dll","lregdll.dll","PerfOS.dll","PerfWatson.exe"]);
let PersistMarkers = dynamic(["Apex","svcghost","EncMgr","PerfWatson","AutoMsg"]);
let Drops =
    ImFileEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(Lookback)
    | where EventType in ("FileCreated","FileRenamed")
    | where (TargetFilePath has_any (StageDirs)) or (TargetFileName in~ (SlowStepperArtifacts))
    | where ActorUsername !endswith "$"
    | where ActingProcessName has_any ("setup","install","vpn","ipany","update")
         or ActingProcessCommandLine has_any (@"\AppData\Local\Temp\", @"\Windows\Temp\", @"\Downloads\")
    | project DropTime = TimeGenerated, DvcHostname,
              DroppedFile = TargetFilePath, FileSHA256 = TargetFileSHA256,
              Dropper = ActingProcessName, DropperCmd = ActingProcessCommandLine,
              ActorUsername;
let Persist =
    union
    ( ImRegistryEvent
      | where TimeGenerated > ago(Lookback)
      | where RegistryKey has_any (@"\CurrentVersion\Run", @"\CurrentVersion\RunOnce", @"\Image File Execution Options\")
      | where RegistryValueData has_any (PersistMarkers)
      | project PersistTime = TimeGenerated, DvcHostname,
                PersistType = "Registry",
                PersistDetail = strcat(RegistryKey, "::", RegistryValue, " = ", RegistryValueData) ),
    ( ImProcessCreate
      | where TimeGenerated > ago(Lookback)
      | where TargetProcessName has "schtasks.exe" and TargetProcessCommandLine has "/create"
      | where TargetProcessCommandLine has_any (PersistMarkers)
      | project PersistTime = TimeGenerated, DvcHostname,
                PersistType = "ScheduledTask",
                PersistDetail = TargetProcessCommandLine );
Drops
| join kind=inner Persist on DvcHostname
| where PersistTime between (DropTime .. DropTime + 1h)
| project DropTime, PersistTime,
          MinutesDelta = datetime_diff('minute', PersistTime, DropTime),
          DvcHostname, ActorUsername, DroppedFile, FileSHA256,
          Dropper, DropperCmd, PersistType, PersistDetail
| order by DropTime desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: PlushDaemon SlowStepper Artifact Drop to %PROGRAMDATA%\Apex
id: 8a3f2b1e-4c2d-4d3a-9d7c-9e6f5e2c1a4b
status: experimental
description: Detects creation of PlushDaemon SlowStepper backdoor artifacts (svcghost.exe, AutoMsg.dll, EncMgr.dll, lregdll.dll, PerfOS.dll) or any write into the actor's characteristic %PROGRAMDATA%\Apex staging directory by an installer-like dropper.
references:
    - https://www.welivesecurity.com/en/eset-research/plushdaemon-compromises-software-supply-chain-south-korean-vpn-service/
author: detection-engineering
date: 2025/01/22
tags:
    - attack.initial_access
    - attack.persistence
    - attack.t1195.002
    - attack.t1574.002
logsource:
    category: file_event
    product: windows
detection:
    selection_path:
        TargetFilename|contains:
            - '\ProgramData\Apex\'
    selection_name:
        TargetFilename|endswith:
            - '\svcghost.exe'
            - '\AutoMsg.dll'
            - '\EncMgr.dll'
            - '\lregdll.dll'
            - '\PerfOS.dll'
            - '\PerfWatson.exe'
    filter_legit:
        Image|endswith:
            - '\MsMpEng.exe'
            - '\TiWorker.exe'
    condition: (selection_path or selection_name) and not filter_legit
falsepositives:
    - Legitimate software using a folder literally named 'Apex' (uncommon outside gaming installers — verify vendor)
level: high
ucs.rationale: PlushDaemon's hallmark in the IPany VPN compromise (and earlier hijacked-update operations against East-Asian targets since 2019) is the very specific staging path %PROGRAMDATA%\Apex and the svcghost / AutoMsg / EncMgr file naming convention used to host the SlowStepper loader and its sideloaded modules. ESET observed the same artifact set across multiple unrelated victims, meaning the names thems
